action.reasoning: Observation: The LoRA checkpoint is now packaged, but eval still reports all predictions missing/invalid while local val sometimes shows only a few valid outputs; this points to inference failure inside predict.py/model.py rather than pure model quality. The current model.py in workspace is truncated and likely has incomplete parser/generation logic, and eval metrics with all missing indicate exceptions or empty outputs during prediction. Analysis: With only 6 iterations left, the highest-value move is not more training but to fully rewrite the inference stack into a compact, self-contained, robust implementation: reliable base-model discovery, structured multimodal chat prompt, robust text parser handling many coordinate formats, and guaranteed point fallback so every sample yields a valid prediction. Then smoke-test on val exactly via predict.py and submit eval immediately. Decision: rewrite model.py and predict.py cleanly, add an inspection script to verify generations on a few samples, run local val prediction/scoring plus a small inspection, and if valid predictions are emitted, submit eval using the packaged LoRA checkpoint.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint /workspace/checkpoints/qwen_lora_small --output val_pred_qwen_portable.json --limit 64 && python score_val.py --pred val_pred_qwen_portable.json --gt /data/unified_val.jsonl && python inspect_outputs.py --data_path /data --checkpoint /workspace/checkpoints/qwen_lora_small --limit 6
